Core:AbsValue: Returns a number's size without its sign, so -5 becomes 5.
Core:AccelerateVelocityX: Smoothly eases horizontal speed toward a target, giving gradual acceleration and braking.
Core:AccelerateVelocityY: Smoothly eases vertical speed toward a target value over time.
Core:ActionAddEvent: Binds a new key, button, or input to a named action at runtime, for remapping.
Core:ActionEraseEvents: Removes all key and button bindings from a named input action.
Core:ActionEventCount: Returns how many keys or buttons are bound to a named action.
Core:ActionHasEvents: True when the named input action has at least one key or button bound.
Core:AddChild: Attaches another node as a child of this one at runtime, e.g. spawning a bullet.
Core:AddToGroup: Tags a node into a named group so you can find or affect it later.
Core:AddVar: Adds an amount to a variable, e.g. increasing score or health.
Core:AddVelocity: Adds a Vector2 to the current velocity, handy for nudges, knockback or boosts.
Core:Always: Always true, so its actions run every time the event is checked.
Core:AngleDifference: Returns the shortest signed turn from one angle to another, in radians.
Core:AngleToPoint: Returns the angle from this node toward a target position, handy for aiming.
Core:AppendLabelText: Adds more text onto the end of a label's existing text.
Core:AppendTextFile: Adds text to the end of an existing file without erasing it (handy for logs).
Core:ApplyCentralForce2D: Applies a continuous push to a rigid body each physics frame, like steady thrust.
Core:ApplyCentralImpulse: Gives a rigid body an instant push in a direction, like a kick or explosion.
Core:ApplyCentralImpulse3D: Gives a 3D physics body a sudden push (e.g. a knockback or launch).
Core:ApplyGravity: Pulls the character downward each frame but caps the maximum falling speed.
Core:ApplyGravitySimple: Adds constant downward acceleration to the character each frame, making it fall.
Core:ApplyTorqueImpulse2D: Gives a rigid body an instant spin, making it start rotating.
Core:ArrayAppend: Adds a value to the end of an array variable.
Core:ArrayAppendArray: Adds every item from another array onto the end of this one.
Core:ArrayAt: Gives the item stored at a specific position in the array.
Core:ArrayBack: Returns the last item in the array.
Core:ArrayClear: Empties an array, removing every item.
Core:ArrayContains: True when the array holds the given value somewhere.
Core:ArrayCount: Returns how many times a value appears in the array.
Core:ArrayDuplicate: Returns an independent copy of the array so edits don't affect the original.
Core:ArrayErase: Removes the first item in the array that matches a given value.
Core:ArrayFill: Sets every slot in the array to the same value.
Core:ArrayFind: Returns the position of a value in the array, or -1 if it's missing.
Core:ArrayFront: Returns the first item in the array.
Core:ArrayInsert: Inserts a value into an array at a specific position.
Core:ArrayIsEmpty: True when the array has no items at all.
Core:ArrayJoin: Joins an array of strings into one text using a separator.
Core:ArrayMax: Returns the largest value found in the array.
Core:ArrayMin: Returns the smallest value found in the array.
Core:ArrayPickRandom: Gives one random item picked from the array.
Core:ArrayPopBack: Removes and returns the last item of the array.
Core:ArrayPopFront: Removes and returns the first item of the array.
Core:ArrayPushFront: Inserts a value at the start of the array, shifting the rest along.
Core:ArrayRemoveAt: Removes the item at a specific position in an array.
Core:ArrayResize: Changes the array's length, adding empty slots or trimming items.
Core:ArrayReverse: Flips the array so its items run in the opposite order.
Core:ArrayShuffle: Randomly reorders the items in an array.
Core:ArraySize: Gives how many items the array currently holds.
Core:ArraySlice: Returns a sub-section of the array between the start and end indexes.
Core:ArraySort: Sorts an array's items into ascending order.
Core:Assert: Crashes during testing if a condition isn't true, catching bugs early; removed from release.
Core:Atan2Value: Returns the angle (in radians) pointing toward a given y and x direction.
Core:AudioGetPosition: Gives the current playback time of this audio player, in seconds.
Core:AudioIsPlaying: True when this audio player is currently making sound.
Core:AudioPlay: Starts this audio player, optionally from a given time in seconds.
Core:AudioPlayStream: Loads an audio file into this player and starts playing it.
Core:AudioSeek: Jumps this audio player's playback to a specific time in seconds.
Core:AudioSetPitch: Changes this player's speed and pitch (1 = normal, higher = faster).
Core:AudioSetVolume: Sets how loud this audio player is, in decibels (0 = full, -80 = silent).
Core:AudioStop: Stops this audio player from playing right now.
Core:AverageInGroup: Returns the average of a numeric property across all members of a group.
Core:AwaitIfOverBudget: Yields to the next frame only if this frame's time budget is used up.
Core:AwaitNextFrame: Pauses this event until the next game frame, to spread work out.
Core:AwaitSignal: Pauses this event until a chosen signal fires, like a timer finishing.
Core:BeginFrameBudget: Starts a per-frame time budget for the loop that follows, to avoid stutter.
Core:BreakJoint: Breaks a joint by clearing its second body, e.g. snapping a rope or chain.
Core:BreakJoint3D: Snaps a 3D joint apart by clearing its second body, releasing the connection.
Core:Breakpoint: Pauses the game in the debugger right here so you can inspect things.
Core:CallAfterDelay: Schedules a method to run later without pausing this event.
Core:CallFunction: Calls one of your sheet functions with arguments, for reusing logic across events.
Core:CallGroup: Calls the named method on every node in a group at once.
Core:CallMethod: Calls a method on a node when you need something not in the menus.
Core:CallMethodValue: Calls a method on a node and uses the value it returns.
Core:CeilValue: Rounds a number up to the nearest whole number.
Core:ChangeScene: Switches the game to a different scene file, replacing the current one.
Core:Choose: Randomly picks one value from a comma-separated list you provide.
Core:ClampFloatValue: Keeps a number from going below a minimum or above a maximum.
Core:ClampValue: Keeps a value within a min and max, clipping anything outside the range.
Core:ClearLineEdit: Empties a single-line text field of all its text.
Core:ClearMaterial: Removes any material from this node, returning it to default drawing.
Core:ColorDarken: Returns the colour shifted toward black by the given amount.
Core:ColorFromHSV: Builds a colour from hue, saturation, value and alpha components.
Core:ColorFromHex: Builds a colour from an HTML hex string like #ff8800.
Core:ColorInverted: Returns the opposite colour, useful for highlight or negative effects.
Core:ColorLerp: Blends two colours by a 0..1 weight for smooth colour fades.
Core:ColorLighten: Returns the colour shifted toward white by the given amount.
Core:ColorWithAlpha: Returns the colour with a new transparency, for fade-in or fade-out effects.
Core:CompareValues: True when two values match your chosen comparison, like equal, greater or less than.
Core:CompareVar: True when a variable compares against a value as you specify, for branching on game state.
Core:ConnectSignal: Wires a node's signal to run a method whenever it fires.
Core:CopyFile: Copies a file from one path to another, leaving the original in place.
Core:CosValue: Returns the cosine of an angle in radians, handy for waves and circular motion.
Core:CurrentItem: Gives you the item the loop is currently working on inside a For Each.
Core:DegToRad: Converts an angle from degrees into radians, which Godot uses internally.
Core:DegToRadValue: Converts an angle from degrees into radians for math functions.
Core:DeleteFile: Permanently deletes a file (or an empty folder) from disk.
Core:DictClear: Empties a dictionary variable, removing every key and value.
Core:DictDeleteKey: Removes a key and its value from a dictionary variable.
Core:DictDuplicate: Returns an independent copy of the dictionary.
Core:DictGet: Reads a key's value, returning your fallback when the key is missing.
Core:DictHasAllKeys: True when the dictionary contains every key in the given list.
Core:DictHasKey: True when the dictionary contains the given key.
Core:DictHasValue: True when the dictionary contains the given value anywhere.
Core:DictIsEmpty: True when the dictionary has no keys at all.
Core:DictKeys: Gives a list of all the dictionary's keys.
Core:DictMerge: Copies another dictionary's keys into this one, overwriting any clashes.
Core:DictSetKey: Stores a value under a key in a dictionary variable, adding or overwriting it.
Core:DictSize: Gives how many keys the dictionary currently holds.
Core:DictValues: Gives a list of all the dictionary's values.
Core:DirExists: True when a folder exists at that path, useful before creating or listing it.
Core:DisableCollisionShape: Switches this collision shape off, safely, so it stops colliding.
Core:DisconnectSignal: Stops a signal from calling a method, so that response no longer fires.
Core:DistanceTo: Returns the distance in pixels from this node to a target position.
Core:DivideVar: Divides a variable by a value, e.g. halving a stat.
Core:DuplicateNode: Clones a node so you can add the copy elsewhere, e.g. mass-spawning identical objects.
Core:EaseValue: Bends a 0-to-1 value along an easing curve for smoother eased motion.
Core:EmitParticlesInObject: Turns the object's particle emitter on or off, found automatically.
Core:EmitSignal: Fires a signal so other events or nodes can react, the way to broadcast custom events.
Core:EmitSignalOn: Fires a signal on an object to notify everything listening for it.
Core:EnableCollisionShape: Switches this collision shape back on so it can collide again.
Core:EvaluateExpression: Returns the result of any GDScript expression you type in.
Core:EvaluateGDScript: True when your own GDScript boolean expression evaluates to true.
Core:EventAsText: Returns a readable label for an input event, like Space or Left Mouse.
Core:EventMatchesAction: True when an input event matches the named action, like checking for jump.
Core:EveryXSeconds: True once each time the chosen number of seconds passes, for repeating timers.
Core:ExpressionIsTrue: True when your custom GDScript expression evaluates to true; an escape hatch for advanced checks.
Core:FileExists: True when a file exists at that path, so you can check before reading or writing it.
Core:FindChild: Returns a child node matching a name pattern, useful when paths vary.
Core:FindChildrenByPattern: Returns all descendant nodes whose name matches a pattern, wildcards allowed.
Core:FindChildrenOfType: Returns all descendant nodes of a given class, e.g. every Area2D beneath this one.
Core:FirstChildOfType: Returns the first descendant node of a given class, or nothing if none exist.
Core:FlipSpriteInObject: Mirrors the object's sprite horizontally, e.g. flipping to face left or right.
Core:FloorValue: Rounds a number down to the nearest whole number.
Core:FmodValue: Returns the remainder after dividing one number by another.
Core:FocusNext: Moves keyboard focus to the next control in tab order.
Core:FocusPrevious: Moves keyboard focus to the previous control in tab order.
Core:FormatString: Builds a text string by filling placeholders with your values, like scores.
Core:FormatTime: Turns a number of seconds into a tidy mm:ss string for timers and clocks.
Core:FurthestInGroup: Returns the farthest member of a group from this node by distance.
Core:GamepadConnected: True when a gamepad at the given device slot is plugged in.
Core:GetActionStrength: Gives how hard an input action is pressed, from 0 to 1.
Core:GetBusVolume: Gives the current volume of a named audio bus, in decibels.
Core:GetButtonText: Returns the label text currently shown on the button.
Core:GetChild: Returns a node's child at the given position number, starting from zero.
Core:GetChildCount: Returns how many direct children a node currently has.
Core:GetChildren: Returns the list of a node's direct children to loop over or pick from.
Core:GetClipboard: Returns whatever text is currently on the system clipboard.
Core:GetCurrentAnimation: Returns the name of the animation currently playing.
Core:GetCurrentState: Returns the name of the state machine's current animation state.
Core:GetDatetimeString: Gives the system's current date and time as readable text.
Core:GetDelta: Returns the seconds since last frame, used to make motion frame-rate independent.
Core:GetFileSize: Returns a file's size in bytes, or zero if the file doesn't exist.
Core:GetFirstNodeInGroup: Returns the first node found in a named group, or nothing if empty.
Core:GetFloorNormal: Returns the direction the floor is facing, useful on slopes.
Core:GetFloorNormal3D: Fires with the slope direction of the floor a 3D body is standing on, handy for slope-aware movement.
Core:GetFps: Gives the current frames per second, useful for performance checks.
Core:GetFrameCount: Gives how many frames have run since startup.
Core:GetGameTime: Gives seconds elapsed since the game started, handy for timers.
Core:GetIndexInParent: Returns the node's position among its siblings as a number.
Core:GetInputAxis: Gives a -1 to 1 value from two opposing input actions, like left and right.
Core:GetInputVector: Returns a movement direction from four input actions, ideal for player movement.
Core:GetJoyAxis: Returns how far a gamepad stick or trigger is pushed, from -1 to 1.
Core:GetLabelText: Returns the text currently displayed on the label.
Core:GetLastSlideCollider: Returns the node the character bumped into last, or nothing if none.
Core:GetLastSlideNormal: Returns the surface direction from the character's last collision.
Core:GetLineEditText: Returns whatever text the player has typed into the field.
Core:GetLinearVelocity2D: Returns the rigid body's current linear velocity from physics.
Core:GetMeta: Returns a custom metadata value previously stored on an object.
Core:GetMonitoring: Returns whether the area is currently watching for overlaps.
Core:GetMouseScreenPosition: Returns the mouse position in screen pixels relative to the window.
Core:GetMouseWorldPosition: Returns the mouse position in world coordinates, matching where things sit in the level.
Core:GetNavDistance: Returns how far the agent still is from its navigation target.
Core:GetNextPathPosition: Returns the next point along the path the agent should move toward.
Core:GetNode: Looks up another node by its scene path so you can use it.
Core:GetNodeCountInGroup: Returns how many nodes are currently in the named group.
Core:GetNodeName: Returns the node's name as text, useful for labels or matching.
Core:GetNodeOrNull: Returns the node at a path, or nothing instead of erroring if missing.
Core:GetNodePath: Returns the node's full path in the scene tree as a reference.
Core:GetNodesInGroup: Returns every node belonging to a named group to loop over or count.
Core:GetOSName: Gives the name of the operating system the game is running on.
Core:GetOverlappingAreas: Returns the list of areas currently overlapping this Area2D.
Core:GetOverlappingBodies: Returns the list of physics bodies currently inside this Area2D.
Core:GetOverlappingBodies3D: Fires with the list of physics bodies currently inside this 3D Area.
Core:GetOwner: Returns the scene that this node was saved as part of.
Core:GetParent: Returns the node directly above this one in the scene tree.
Core:GetParticleAmount: Returns how many particles the emitter is set to spawn.
Core:GetPerfMonitor: Returns a live engine performance reading, like FPS or memory, for debugging.
Core:GetPlaybackPosition: Returns how many seconds into the sound the audio player currently is.
Core:GetPosition2D: Returns the node's 2D position as a Vector2.
Core:GetPosition3D: Returns a 3D node's current world position as a Vector3.
Core:GetProperty: Reads the current value of any property on any node.
Core:GetRandomNodeInGroup: Returns a randomly chosen member of a named group, e.g. a random spawn point.
Core:GetRangeRatio: Returns the value as a 0-to-1 ratio, handy for filling bars.
Core:GetRangeValue: Returns the current value of a slider, progress bar, or spinbox.
Core:GetSceneRoot: Returns the root node of the currently running scene.
Core:GetScreenSize: Returns the size of the player's monitor in pixels.
Core:GetShaderParameter: Gives the current value of a named shader uniform on this node.
Core:GetSlideCount: Returns how many things the character hit during its last move.
Core:GetSpriteAnimation: Returns the name of the animation the sprite is currently using.
Core:GetStaticMemory: Returns how much memory the game is currently using, in bytes.
Core:GetSystemDate: Returns the player's current calendar date as a text string.
Core:GetSystemTime: Returns the player's current clock time as a text string.
Core:GetTimeScale: Gives the current game speed (1 = normal, below 1 = slow motion).
Core:GetTimerTimeLeft: Returns the seconds remaining before the timer fires.
Core:GetTouchPosition: Returns the screen position of a touch from the current input event.
Core:GetTreeParam: Returns the current value of an AnimationTree parameter.
Core:GetUnixTime: Gives the current Unix timestamp in seconds, useful for saving real-world time.
Core:GetVar: Returns the current value stored in the named variable.
Core:GetVelocity2D: Returns the character body's current movement velocity.
Core:GetVelocity3D: Returns a 3D character body's current velocity vector.
Core:GetVelocityX: Returns the character's current horizontal speed in pixels per second.
Core:GetVelocityY: Returns the character's current vertical speed in pixels per second.
Core:GetWallNormal: Returns the direction the touched wall is facing, for wall-jumps or sliding.
Core:GetWallNormal3D: Fires with the direction a 3D body just bumped into a wall, useful for wall-jumps or ricochets.
Core:GetWindowHeight: Gives the current window height in pixels.
Core:GetWindowSize: Returns the game window's current size in pixels.
Core:GetWindowWidth: Gives the current window width in pixels.
Core:GrabFocus: Gives this control keyboard focus, so input goes to it next.
Core:HasChildOfType: True when at least one descendant node of the given class exists beneath this node.
Core:HasFocus: True when this control currently holds keyboard focus.
Core:HasGroupMember: True when this node belongs to the named group, for tagging and identifying objects.
Core:HasMeta: True when the object has metadata stored under the given key.
Core:HasNode: True when a node exists at the given path under this one.
Core:HasOSFeature: True when the current platform supports the given feature tag, like mobile or web.
Core:HasOverlappingAreas: True when this Area2D currently overlaps any other area.
Core:HasOverlappingBodies: True when this Area2D currently overlaps any physics body.
Core:HasOverlappingBodies3D: True when this 3D Area is currently overlapping at least one physics body.
Core:HideNode: Hides a node so it no longer shows on screen.
Core:InlineIf: Picks one of two values depending on a condition, all in one line.
Core:InverseLerp: Returns where a value sits within a range as a 0-to-1 fraction.
Core:IsActionJustPressed: True only on the frame the named input action was first pressed, for jumps or single taps.
Core:IsActionJustReleased: True only on the frame the named input action was let go, for charge-and-release moves.
Core:IsActionPressed: True while the named input action is held down, for continuous controls like running.
Core:IsAncestorOf: True when this node is somewhere above the other node in the tree.
Core:IsAnimationPlaying: True while the AnimationPlayer is playing an animation.
Core:IsAudioPlaying: True while the audio player is currently playing a sound.
Core:IsBetween: True when a value falls within a low and high range, bounds included.
Core:IsButtonDisabled: True when this button is disabled and can't be clicked.
Core:IsButtonPressed: True while this button is currently pressed or toggled on.
Core:IsEmitting: True when the particle emitter is currently emitting particles.
Core:IsEqualApprox: True when two numbers are nearly equal, avoiding tiny floating-point errors.
Core:IsGroupActive: True when the named runtime group is currently switched on.
Core:IsInGroup: True when the given node currently belongs to the named group.
Core:IsInState: True when the state machine is currently in the named animation state.
Core:IsInsideTree: True when the node is currently part of the active scene tree.
Core:IsNavFinished: True once the navigation agent has reached its target destination.
Core:IsNull: True when the value is null, meaning nothing or missing.
Core:IsObjectAnimating: True when any AnimationPlayer beneath the object is currently playing.
Core:IsOnCeiling: True when this 2D character is touching a ceiling above.
Core:IsOnCeiling3D: True when this 3D character is touching a ceiling above.
Core:IsOnCollisionLayer: True when this object occupies the given collision layer.
Core:IsOnFloor: True when this 2D character body is standing on the ground, used to gate jumping.
Core:IsOnFloor3D: True when a 3D character body is standing on the ground (check before jumping).
Core:IsOnWall: True when this 2D character is pressing against a wall.
Core:IsOnWall3D: True when this 3D character is pressing against a wall.
Core:IsPaused: True when the game is currently paused.
Core:IsSignalConnected: True when a method is currently hooked up to listen for that signal.
Core:IsSpriteAnimationPlaying: True while the animated sprite is currently playing an animation.
Core:IsTimerStopped: True when the Timer is not currently running.
Core:IsTouchscreen: True when the device running the game has a touchscreen.
Core:IsTreeActive: True when this AnimationTree is currently active and playing.
Core:IsValid: True when the object still exists and hasn't been freed.
Core:IsVisible: True when the node is currently visible on screen.
Core:IsZeroApprox: True when a value is essentially zero, ignoring tiny rounding differences.
Core:JoyButtonDown: True while the given gamepad button is being held down.
Core:JsonIsValid: True when the given text is valid JSON, so you can check before parsing it.
Core:JsonLoadFile: Reads a JSON file and parses it straight into a variable.
Core:JsonParse: Reads JSON text back into a usable value, returning nothing if the text is invalid.
Core:JsonParseToVar: Parses JSON text and stores the result in a variable (null if the text is bad).
Core:JsonSaveFile: Serializes a value to pretty JSON and writes it to a file in one step.
Core:JsonStringify: Turns a value like a dictionary or array into compact JSON text for saving or sending.
Core:JsonStringifyPretty: Turns a value into neatly indented JSON text that's easy for humans to read.
Core:KeyEventPressed: True the moment a key is pressed, used inside an input event.
Core:KeyEventReleased: True the moment a key is released, used inside an input event.
Core:KeyIsDown: True while the given keyboard key is being held down.
Core:LerpAngle: Blends between two angles by a 0..1 weight, taking the shortest path.
Core:LerpValue: Blends between two values by a 0-to-1 weight, for smooth interpolation.
Core:ListDirs: Returns the list of subfolder names inside a folder.
Core:ListFiles: Returns the list of file names inside a folder (empty if the folder is missing).
Core:LoadResource: Loads a scene or resource from a res:// path so you can use it.
Core:LoadSettingInto: Reads a saved value from a config file into a variable, with a fallback default.
Core:LookAt3D: Turns a 3D node to face a world position (e.g. an enemy facing the player).
Core:LoopBreak: Stops the current loop early and skips any remaining items.
Core:LoopContinue: Skips to the next item in the loop, ignoring the rest of this pass.
Core:MakeCamera3DCurrent: Switches the view to this 3D camera, making it the active one.
Core:MakeCameraCurrent: Makes this camera the active one the player views the game through.
Core:MakeDir: Creates a folder, building any missing parent folders along the way.
Core:MakeVector2: Builds a Vector2 from separate x and y numbers, for positions or directions.
Core:MakeVector3: Builds a 3D point or direction from X, Y and Z numbers.
Core:MaxInGroup: Returns the largest value of a property among all group members.
Core:MaxValue: Returns whichever of two numbers is larger.
Core:MinInGroup: Returns the smallest value of a property among all group members.
Core:MinValue: Returns whichever of two numbers is smaller.
Core:MouseButtonDown: True while the given mouse button is being held down.
Core:MoveAndSlide: Moves the character body using its velocity and slides along walls; call each physics frame.
Core:MoveAndSlide3D: Moves a 3D character body by its velocity, sliding smoothly along walls and slopes.
Core:MoveBy2D: Shifts a 2D node by an offset from where it is, for simple step-based movement.
Core:MoveChild: Reorders a child to a new sibling index, changing its draw and process order.
Core:MoveFile: Moves or renames a file (or folder) to a new path.
Core:MoveTowardValue: Nudges a value toward a target by a set step, great for smooth changes.
Core:MultiplyVar: Multiplies a variable by a factor, e.g. scaling speed or applying a bonus.
Core:NearestInGroup: Returns the closest member of a group to this node, e.g. the nearest enemy.
Core:OnAnimationFinished: Runs when an animation finishes playing, e.g. chaining the next animation or action.
Core:OnAreaEntered: Runs when another 2D Area overlaps this one, e.g. a hitbox touching a hurtbox.
Core:OnAreaExited: Runs when another 2D Area stops overlapping this one.
Core:OnBodyEntered: Runs when a physics body enters this 2D Area, e.g. detecting the player walking into a trigger.
Core:OnBodyExited: Runs when a physics body leaves this 2D Area, e.g. the player stepping out of a zone.
Core:OnButtonPressed: Runs when the player clicks or activates this button.
Core:OnButtonToggled: Runs when a toggle button is switched on or off.
Core:OnChildEnteredTree: Runs when a child node is added beneath this one, e.g. reacting to spawned items.
Core:OnEditorRun: Runs inside the editor while building, useful for tool scripts and live previews.
Core:OnInput: Runs on every input event the node receives, for catching keys, mouse, or touch.
Core:OnParticlesFinished: Fires once when this particle emitter's one-shot burst finishes playing.
Core:OnPhysicsProcess: Runs every fixed physics step, the right place for physics-based movement and forces.
Core:OnProcess: Runs every rendered frame, perfect for continuous movement, timers, or polling input.
Core:OnReady: Runs once when this node first enters the scene, ideal for setup and initial values.
Core:OnRenamed: Runs when this node's name changes in the scene tree.
Core:OnSignal: Runs whenever the named signal fires, letting you react to any custom or built-in event.
Core:OnTimeout: Runs when this Timer counts down to zero, e.g. ending a cooldown or spawn delay.
Core:OnTreeEntered: Runs when this node is added into the scene tree.
Core:OnTreeExited: Runs after this node has been removed from the scene tree.
Core:OnTreeExiting: Runs just before this node leaves the scene tree, a good spot for cleanup.
Core:OnUnhandledInput: Runs on input no UI element consumed, ideal for gameplay controls that ignore menu clicks.
Core:OverlapsArea: True when this Area2D is overlapping the given other area.
Core:OverlapsBody: True when this Area2D is overlapping the given physics body.
Core:PingPong: Bounces a value back and forth between 0 and a length, great for looping motion.
Core:PlayAnimation: Plays a named animation on an AnimationPlayer, e.g. for walking or attacking.
Core:PlayAnimationInObject: Plays a named animation by auto-finding the object's AnimationPlayer for you.
Core:PlayAudio: Plays the sound on an audio player, optionally starting from a given second.
Core:PlaySound: Plays a sound file once on a chosen bus and volume, then cleans itself up.
Core:PlaySoundAt: Plays a sound at a world position so it gets louder or quieter with distance.
Core:PlaySpriteAnimation: Plays a named animation on an animated sprite (e.g. run or jump).
Core:PlaySpriteAnimationInObject: Plays a named sprite animation via the object's AnimatedSprite2D, found automatically.
Core:PosMod: Returns a modulo result that stays positive, handy for wrapping indexes.
Core:PowValue: Raises a base number to an exponent power, like 2 to the 8th.
Core:Print: Prints a value to the Output console, useful for debugging what's happening.
Core:PrintLabeled: Prints a value preceded by a label so you can tell debug messages apart.
Core:PrintLog: Prints a message to the output console, useful for debugging and checking values.
Core:PrintRich: Prints colored or bold text to the Output console using BBCode formatting.
Core:PrintTree: Prints the whole scene's node hierarchy to the output log for debugging.
Core:PushError: Logs an error message that appears in Godot's debugger panel.
Core:PushWarning: Logs a warning message that appears in Godot's debugger panel.
Core:QueueFree: Removes this node safely at the end of the frame, e.g. destroying a defeated enemy.
Core:QueueFreeNode: Safely deletes a node at the end of the frame, e.g. removing a dead enemy.
Core:QuitGame: Closes the game and exits to desktop.
Core:RadToDeg: Converts an angle from radians back into easy-to-read degrees.
Core:RadToDegValue: Converts an angle from radians back into easier-to-read degrees.
Core:RandomInt: Returns a random whole number between the two bounds, both included.
Core:RandomRange: Returns a random decimal number between the two bounds you give.
Core:RandomizeSeed: Reseeds randomness from the clock so each playthrough differs.
Core:RayCast2DForceUpdate: Immediately re-checks the raycast this frame instead of waiting for physics.
Core:RayCast2DGetCollider: Returns the node the raycast is currently hitting, or nothing if clear.
Core:RayCast2DGetNormal: Returns the surface direction (normal) at the raycast's hit point.
Core:RayCast2DGetPoint: Returns the world point where the raycast hit something.
Core:RayCast2DIsColliding: True when the RayCast2D is currently hitting something in its path.
Core:RayCast3DForceUpdate: Forces a RayCast3D to recheck immediately instead of waiting for the next frame.
Core:RayCast3DGetCollider: Returns the object a RayCast3D is currently hitting.
Core:RayCast3DGetNormal: Returns the surface direction at the point a RayCast3D hits.
Core:RayCast3DGetPoint: Returns the exact world point where a RayCast3D hits something.
Core:RayCast3DIsColliding: True when a RayCast3D is currently hitting something in front of it.
Core:ReadTextFile: Returns the whole file's contents as text (empty if it's missing or unreadable).
Core:ReleaseFocus: Removes keyboard focus from this control.
Core:ReloadScene: Restarts the current scene from scratch, useful for retrying a level.
Core:RemapValue: Rescales a number from one range into another, like mapping 0-100 onto 0-1.
Core:RemoveChild: Detaches a child node from this one without deleting it, so you can reattach it later.
Core:RemoveDir: Deletes an empty folder (clear out its files first).
Core:RemoveFromGroup: Untags a node from a named group when it should no longer belong.
Core:RemoveMeta: Deletes a stored metadata value from an object by its key.
Core:ReparentNode: Moves this node under a new parent while keeping its on-screen position.
Core:RestartAnimationInObject: Replays a named animation from the very start, e.g. retriggering an attack.
Core:RestartParticles: Restarts the particle system from scratch, e.g. firing a fresh burst.
Core:RestartParticlesCPU: Restarts a CPU particle system from scratch, e.g. firing a fresh burst.
Core:ReturnEarly: Exits the current function immediately, skipping any remaining actions.
Core:ReturnValue: Returns a value from the current function back to whatever called it.
Core:RotateNode3D: Spins a 3D node around an axis by an angle, often using speed times delta.
Core:RotateTowardAngle: Steps an angle toward a target by a limited amount, for smooth turning.
Core:RoundValue: Rounds a number to the nearest whole number.
Core:RunGDScript: Drops in one line of raw GDScript for things the menus can't do.
Core:SaveSetting: Writes a value into a config file on disk so it persists between play sessions.
Core:SeedRandom: Sets the random seed so the same number gives a repeatable random sequence.
Core:SetAnchorsPreset: Snaps a control's anchors to a layout preset like full-rect or center.
Core:SetAnimationTreeActive: Turns this AnimationTree's playback on or off.
Core:SetBusMute: Mutes or unmutes a named audio bus all at once.
Core:SetBusVolume: Sets the volume of a named audio bus, like Music or SFX.
Core:SetButtonDisabled: Enables or disables a button so it can or can't be clicked.
Core:SetButtonPressedState: Sets a toggle button's pressed state without firing its toggled event.
Core:SetCameraFov: Sets a 3D camera's field of view in degrees (lower zooms in, higher widens).
Core:SetCameraLimits: Sets the boundaries the camera won't scroll past, keeping it inside the level.
Core:SetCameraOffset: Shifts the camera view away from the position it follows.
Core:SetCameraZoom: Sets how zoomed in or out the camera is.
Core:SetClipboard: Copies text to the operating system clipboard for pasting elsewhere.
Core:SetCollisionLayerBit: Turns a collision layer on or off, controlling what this object sits on.
Core:SetCollisionMaskBit: Turns a collision mask bit on or off, controlling what this object detects.
Core:SetDampedSpringDamping: Sets how quickly a damped spring stops bouncing, controlling its wobble.
Core:SetDampedSpringLength: Sets a spring joint's resting length, the distance it tries to hold.
Core:SetDampedSpringStiffness: Sets how rigid a damped spring joint feels, so it snaps back harder or softer.
Core:SetEmitting: Starts or stops the particle emitter, e.g. switching an effect on.
Core:SetEmittingCPU: Starts or stops a CPU particle emitter, e.g. switching an effect on.
Core:SetFlipH: Mirrors the sprite horizontally, great for facing left or right.
Core:SetFocusNeighbor: Sets which control gets focus when arrowing in a given direction.
Core:SetFullscreen: Switches the game window between windowed, fullscreen and other display modes.
Core:SetGroupActive: Turns a runtime-toggleable group on or off to enable or disable its behaviour.
Core:SetJointBodyA: Sets the first physics body a joint connects to.
Core:SetJointBodyA3D: Picks the first 3D body a joint connects, wiring up what it links to.
Core:SetJointBodyB: Sets the second physics body a joint connects to.
Core:SetJointBodyB3D: Picks the second 3D body a joint connects, completing the link.
Core:SetJointDisableCollision: Toggles whether the two bodies linked by the joint can collide with each other.
Core:SetLabelText: Sets the text shown on a label, like a score or message.
Core:SetLineEditText: Sets the text shown in a single-line text field.
Core:SetLocalFromAxis: Reads a left/right input axis into a local variable for this event.
Core:SetLocalVar: Creates a temporary variable used only within this event.
Core:SetLocalVarTyped: Creates a temporary variable of a fixed type within this event.
Core:SetMeta: Stores a custom named value on an object as hidden metadata.
Core:SetModulate: Tints a node and its children with a color, also useful for fading via alpha.
Core:SetMouseMode: Changes whether the cursor is visible, hidden, or locked to the window.
Core:SetNavTarget: Tells a navigation agent to pathfind toward a world position, for AI movement.
Core:SetNodeName: Renames a node at runtime, handy for tracking or finding it later.
Core:SetOneShot: Sets the emitter to fire a single burst then stop, rather than looping.
Core:SetParticleAmount: Sets how many particles the emitter spawns, controlling effect density.
Core:SetParticleSpeedScale: Speeds up or slows down the particle effect, e.g. 0 freezes it, 2 doubles it.
Core:SetParticleSpeedScaleCPU: Speeds up or slows down a CPU particle effect, e.g. 0 freezes it, 2 doubles it.
Core:SetPaused: Pauses or resumes the whole game by toggling the scene tree's pause state.
Core:SetPinJointSoftness: Sets how springy a pin joint is, higher values make the link looser.
Core:SetPosition2D: Places a 2D node at an exact position, e.g. teleporting or snapping to a spot.
Core:SetPosition3D: Teleports a 3D node to an exact world position.
Core:SetProperty: Sets any property on any node, like visible, position, or modulate.
Core:SetRangeMax: Sets the maximum value of a slider, progress bar, or spinbox.
Core:SetRangeValue: Sets a slider, progress bar, or spinbox to a specific value.
Core:SetRotationDeg: Sets a 2D node's rotation in degrees, e.g. aiming or facing a direction.
Core:SetRotationDeg3D: Sets a 3D node's rotation directly using degree angles.
Core:SetScale3D: Sets how big a 3D node is by changing its scale.
Core:SetSelfModulate: Tints just this node with a color without affecting its children.
Core:SetShaderMaterial: Assigns a shader or canvas material to this node to change how it draws.
Core:SetShaderParameter: Feeds a value into a shader uniform to drive a visual effect at runtime.
Core:SetSpriteFrame: Jumps the animated sprite to a specific frame number.
Core:SetSpriteFrameInObject: Shows a specific frame on the object's AnimatedSprite2D, found automatically.
Core:SetTextFormatted: Sets a label or button's text using a format string filled with your values.
Core:SetThemeColorOverride: Overrides one theme color on this control, like its font color.
Core:SetTimeScale: Speeds up or slows the whole game; use for slow-motion or pausing.
Core:SetTreeParam: Sets an AnimationTree parameter like a blend amount, condition or timescale.
Core:SetVar: Sets a variable to a value you give, the basic way to store game state.
Core:SetVelocity2D: Sets the character's full movement velocity to the Vector2 you provide.
Core:SetVelocity3D: Sets a 3D character body's velocity, which Move And Slide then uses to move it.
Core:SetVelocityX: Sets only the horizontal speed of the character, leaving vertical motion untouched.
Core:SetVelocityY: Sets only the vertical speed of the character (negative values move upward).
Core:SetVolumeDb: Sets an audio player's loudness in decibels (0 is full, -80 is silent).
Core:SetWindowSize: Resizes the game window to a chosen pixel width and height.
Core:SetWindowTitle: Changes the text shown in the game window's title bar.
Core:ShowNode: Makes a node visible on screen.
Core:SignValue: Returns -1, 0, or 1 to tell whether a number is negative, zero, or positive.
Core:SinValue: Returns the sine of an angle in radians, handy for waves and circular motion.
Core:Smoothstep: Eases a value between two edges with a smooth S-curve instead of a straight line.
Core:Snapped: Rounds a value to the nearest step, useful for snapping to a grid.
Core:SnappedValue: Snaps a value to the nearest multiple of a step, like a grid.
Core:SpawnScene: Loads a scene file and adds an instance of it as a child (spawning objects).
Core:SpawnSceneAt: Loads a scene and drops a copy into the world at a position.
Core:SpawnSceneFull: Spawns a scene copy with position, rotation and an optional group in one step.
Core:SquareRoot: Returns the square root of a number.
Core:StartJoyVibration: Rumbles a connected gamepad at chosen strength for a set duration.
Core:StartTimer: Starts a Timer node counting down, optionally with a custom duration.
Core:StopAnimation: Stops the currently playing animation on the AnimationPlayer.
Core:StopAnimationInObject: Stops the object's animation by auto-finding its AnimationPlayer.
Core:StopAudio: Stops the sound currently playing on an audio player.
Core:StopSpriteAnimation: Stops the animated sprite's current animation on the spot.
Core:StopTimer: Stops a running Timer so it no longer counts down or fires.
Core:StringBeginsWith: True when the text starts with the given prefix.
Core:StringContains: True when the text contains the given substring somewhere inside it.
Core:StringEndsWith: True when the text ends with the given suffix.
Core:StringPadZeros: Returns the number padded with leading zeros, e.g. for scores like 007.
Core:StringRepeat: Returns the text repeated the given number of times.
Core:StringSplit: Returns the text chopped into a list wherever the separator appears.
Core:StringToFloat: Returns the text parsed into a decimal number.
Core:StringToInt: Returns the text parsed into a whole number.
Core:SubtractVar: Subtracts an amount from a variable, e.g. spending money or taking damage.
Core:SumInGroup: Returns the total of a numeric property added up across every group member.
Core:TanValue: Returns the tangent of an angle given in radians.
Core:TextFind: Gives where a substring first appears in text, or -1 if it's missing.
Core:TextLeft: Gives the first few characters from the start of some text.
Core:TextLength: Gives how many characters are in some text.
Core:TextLower: Gives the text converted to all lowercase letters.
Core:TextMid: Gives a chunk of text starting at a position for a set length.
Core:TextReplace: Gives the text with every match of one substring swapped for another.
Core:TextRight: Gives the last few characters from the end of some text.
Core:TextTokenAt: Splits text by a separator and gives the chosen piece, like a CSV column.
Core:TextTokenCount: Gives how many pieces text breaks into when split by a separator.
Core:TextTrim: Gives the text with leading and trailing whitespace removed.
Core:TextUpper: Gives the text converted to all uppercase letters.
Core:TextZeroPad: Gives a number padded with leading zeros to a fixed width, like 007.
Core:TileMapCellHasSource: True when the chosen tilemap cell actually has a tile placed.
Core:TileMapCellIsEmpty: True when the chosen tilemap cell has no tile in it.
Core:TileMapClear: Wipes every tile from the tilemap layer, leaving it blank.
Core:TileMapEraseCell: Clears the tile at a single grid cell, leaving it empty.
Core:TileMapGetCellAtlasCoords: Returns which tile in the atlas sits at the given cell.
Core:TileMapGetCellSourceId: Returns the tile source ID at a cell, or -1 when empty.
Core:TileMapLocalToMap: Converts a pixel position into the cell coordinates that contain it.
Core:TileMapMapToLocal: Converts cell coordinates into the pixel position at that cell's center.
Core:TileMapSetCell: Paints a tile at a grid cell using a tile source and atlas position.
Core:TileMapUsedCellsCount: Returns how many cells in the tilemap currently hold a tile.
Core:ToggleBool: Flips a true/false variable to its opposite value.
Core:TouchEventPressed: True the moment a finger touches the screen, used inside an input event.
Core:TouchEventReleased: True the moment a finger lifts off the screen, used inside an input event.
Core:TranslateNode3D: Nudges a 3D node by an offset relative to its own facing (local space).
Core:TravelToState: Tells the state machine to transition to the named animation state.
Core:TweenCallback: Waits a delay, then calls a method or function once (handy for timed events).
Core:TweenProperty: Smoothly animates a node's property to a target value over time with an easing curve.
Core:TypeOf: Returns a number identifying what kind of value something is.
Core:VectorAngle: Returns a 2D vector's angle in radians, useful for facing direction.
Core:VectorDirectionTo: Returns a unit vector pointing from one point toward another, handy for aiming.
Core:VectorDistanceTo: Returns the straight-line distance between two points.
Core:VectorDot: Returns the dot product of two vectors, telling how aligned they are.
Core:VectorLength: Returns how long a vector is, e.g. a velocity's speed.
Core:VectorLerp: Returns a point blended between two vectors, great for smooth movement.
Core:VectorLimitLength: Returns the vector capped to a maximum length, e.g. a speed limit.
Core:VectorNormalized: Returns the vector shrunk to length 1, keeping only its direction.
Core:VectorRotated: Returns the vector turned by the given angle in radians.
Core:Wait: Pauses this event for a number of seconds before continuing.
Core:WorldRaycastCollider2D: Returns the object a one-shot ray between two points hits, or nothing.
Core:WorldRaycastHit2D: True when a ray drawn between two points hits any physics object.
Core:WorldRaycastHit3D: True when a ray cast between two points hits anything in the 3D world.
Core:WorldRaycastPoint2D: Returns where a one-shot ray between two points strikes a surface.
Core:WorldRaycastPoint3D: Returns the world point where a ray between two points first hits something.
Core:WrapValue: Wraps a value to stay within a range, looping past the edges back around.
Core:WriteTextFile: Saves text to a file, overwriting anything already there (great for save data).
